title: pleopeltis pteropus
intro: Pleopeltis pteropus, also known as the resurrection fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States. It is prized for its unique ability to dry out and revive itself when rehydrated, earning its common name. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis pteropus can be used in teas, infusions, or topical creams to leverage its health benefits, such as accelerating wound healing and soothing irritations.
